description:
Walk through the actual code path from app launch to first transcribed text.
Count: seconds elapsed, decision points (clicks/permissions/navigation), and
pauses >3 seconds. Zero code changes — this is a measurement.

Current prediction: 4+ minutes, 8+ decision points.
Target: <30 seconds, 2 decision points.

Trace: MeetyApp → WelcomeView → auth → OnboardingView → DashboardView → 
Meeting tab → Start → transcription service → first utterance.

Output: .medici/mm-eey/experiment-time-to-transcript.md
title: Experiment: measure time-to-first-transcript baseline on cold install

description:
After ending a session, show a dismissible summary card:
- Session duration
- Audio processed by: Deepgram (cloud) or On-Device (local)
- Coaching provided by: [LLM provider]
- Transcript saved: [local path] — [View] [Delete]
- "No screen content was captured"

One new SwiftUI view (~100 lines). Small data model addition to MeetingSession
to track which services were used. Integration into session-end flow.

Files:
- macos/Meety/Features/Meeting/DataReceiptView.swift (new)
- macos/Meety/Models/MeetingSession.swift (add service tracking)
- macos/Meety/Features/Meeting/MeetingViewModel.swift (show on session end)
title: Experiment: post-session data receipt card

description:
Add a single-line coaching note to the floating session controls panel so users
get coaching information without switching to the main Meety window.

## Requirements
- Show the highest-priority coaching note as a single line (truncated)
- Animate: new notes fade in, replacing the previous
- Auto-expire notes after ~90 seconds unless pinned
- Subtle enough to read in peripheral vision near the meeting window
- Position controls near camera (top-center) by default for video calls

description:
Replace the current multi-indicator header (session status, network status,
transcription service, audio source, reconnection spinner) with a single
compound status indicator.

## Design
- Single colored dot + small icon:
  - Green dot + cloud icon = Deepgram active
  - Green dot + laptop icon = on-device active
  - Orange dot = degraded (service switched, reconnecting)
  - Red dot = broken
- Lives in both floating controls AND menu bar
- Expandable on hover to show detail (which service, connection quality)
- During meeting: this is the ONLY status element
- Error consolidation: concurrent errors → single-line format

## Files
- macos/Meety/Features/Meeting/SessionControlsView.swift
- macos/Meety/Features/MenuBar/MenuBarView.swift
- macos/Meety/Features/Meeting/MeetingView.swift (remove stacked error banners during session)

## Source
Medici analysis: trust×friction collision (temporal trust boundary, during-meeting compression)
title: Replace multi-indicator header with compound health+trust status indicator

description:
Add a dashboardFocusTime counter to MeetingViewModel that tracks how long the
coaching panel receives mouse hover/enter events vs total session time. Log the
ratio. No UI changes — diagnostic metric only.

This answers: how much attention do users actually give the coaching panel today?
If <5%, the three-layer model is validated. If >20%, users may need the full dashboard.

Files: macos/Meety/Features/Meeting/MeetingViewModel.swift
Output: instrumented code ready for real meeting testing
title: Experiment: instrument dashboard focus time during meetings

description:
Redesign CoachingDashboardView content into three attention layers without
changing the container (inspector refactor comes later).

## Layer 1 — Peripheral (always visible, zero reading)
- Single color bar along top edge: green (going well), yellow (aware), red (action needed)
- Derived from case strength + high-severity risk tickets
- No text, no numbers — color and luminance only

## Layer 2 — Glance (200ms look-down)
- At most ONE card visible, priority-ranked: high risk > urgent note > suggested response
- Large type (16pt+), under 12 words, colored left border
- Most recent coaching note uses teleprompter model: new note fades in, previous fades to 30% opacity
- Predicted questions and intent excluded from live meeting display

## Layer 3 — Focus (on deliberate interaction)
- Full dashboard expands on hover/click/keyboard shortcut
- Current CoachingDashboardView layout for this layer
- Suggested responses, document references, closed risk tickets here

## Default state
- Start quiet: peripheral bar + "Listening..." in muted text
- Populate as signals arrive
- Empty panels do not render

## Files
- macos/Meety/Features/Meeting/CoachingDashboardView.swift (major refactor)
- macos/Meety/Features/Meeting/MeetingView.swift (layout integration)

## Source
Medici analysis: calm lens, analogy lens, craft×calm collision
title: Implement three-layer coaching content (peripheral/glance/focus)

description: Give the app outbound gravity. Monitor EventKit for upcoming calendar events. 5 min before a meeting: show macOS notification. Tapping opens app on Meeting tab with session ready. Detect video links (Zoom/Teams/Meet URL) and pre-select audio source. Persistent menu bar shows next meeting time. Respect notification preferences in Settings. Files: CalendarService.swift, NotificationService.swift, MenuBarView.swift, DashboardView.swift. Source: Medici user lens (inter-meeting gap is #1 retention killer).
title: Add calendar-triggered meeting notifications and auto-session prompts

description:
attached_molecule: mm-wisp-fz55
attached_formula: medici-solve
attached_at: 2026-03-18T02:55:43Z
dispatched_by: meety_me/crew/chuck

Design a world-class UI/UX strategy for the Meety Me macOS meeting companion app.

## Current State
- App captures meeting audio via ScreenCaptureKit, provides real-time AI coaching, generates post-meeting summaries
- MVVM architecture with SwiftUI
- Features: onboarding, menu bar presence, floating meeting controls (QuickTime-style), dashboard, settings, chat, document RAG, meeting prep
- Recent work: audio source picker, transcription service indicator, redesigned floating controls

## Known UX Pain Points
1. ScreenCaptureKit permission confusion — users see "Screen Recording" for audio-only capture
2. Browser-based meeting audio capture includes all tabs, not just the meeting
3. WebSocket reconnection gaps when laptop sleeps (Deepgram disconnects)
4. PTT key conflicts with macOS text input during meetings

## Goal
Make this feel like a polished, delightful native macOS app on par with Bear, Things, Fantastical, or Krisp — intuitive enough that users never need documentation, with interactions that feel inevitable rather than designed.

## Existing Analysis
- .specs/plan-ui-improvements.md — 447-line product spec
- .specs/analysis-ux-feasibility.md — Critical UX review
- .specs/plan.md — Full product roadmap with 18+ known bugs/gaps
title: Design world-class UI/UX strategy for Meety Me macOS app

description:
Remove authentication as a prerequisite for experiencing transcription. Allow
unauthenticated users to start a session with local (on-device) transcription.

## Requirements
- OnboardingView leads directly to a demo session after mic permission
- Auth is deferred to when coaching or cloud features are needed
- LocalTranscriptionService via TranscriptionRouter is the default
- First session should produce transcribed text within 2 seconds of speech
- Target: time-to-first-transcript < 30 seconds from app launch

## Files
- macos/Meety/Features/Authentication/WelcomeView.swift (bypass for demo)
- macos/Meety/Features/Onboarding/OnboardingView.swift (flow to session)
- macos/Meety/Application/MeetyApp.swift (routing logic)

## Depends on
TranscriptionRouter (mm-bead TBD — will wire dependency)

## Source
Medici analysis: friction lens (F1), user lens (Phase 1), all 7 lenses converged
title: Enable zero-config first session (no auth gate, local transcription default)

description: Branch polecat/thunder/mm-ngv@mmf2clqf was rejected by refinery (MR mm-wisp-fef). The fix removes attendeeEmails from CalendarEvent init calls but the CalendarEvent struct still has attendeeEmails as a required field — would break compilation. Need to either: (a) keep attendeeEmails in struct and pass it in init calls, or (b) remove it from struct definition too. Rework needed before merge.
title: Fix CalendarEvent attendeeEmails: remove from init calls OR from struct

description:
Create a TranscriptionRouter class conforming to the Transcribing protocol that manages
seamless failover between LocalTranscriptionService and TranscriptionService (Deepgram).

## Requirements
- Start LocalTranscriptionService immediately on connect() (zero-config, instant)
- Attempt Deepgram connection in parallel if API key is configured
- Hot-swap to Deepgram when it connects
- Fall back to local immediately when Deepgram disconnects (no gap)
- Publish @Published var activeProvider: TranscriptionProvider (.local, .deepgram)
- Merge utterance streams from both services
- Failover at session-segment granularity (not mid-utterance)

## Files
- macos/Meety/Services/TranscriptionRouter.swift (new)
- macos/Meety/Features/Meeting/MeetingViewModel.swift (use router instead of individual services)

## Source
Medici analysis: constraints lens + collision synthesis
title: Build TranscriptionRouter with seamless local/Deepgram failover
